audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Thessalonians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for day of Lord, sober watchfulness, armor as faith/love/hope, community practices, testing prophecy, whole sanctification, final grace.

verses.restored: Now concerning the times and the seasons, siblings, you have no need to be written to.
For you yourselves know accurately that the day of the Lord comes unexpectedly, like a visitor in night.
When people say, Peace and security, then sudden reckoning comes upon them, like birth-pain, and it cannot simply be avoided.
But you, siblings, are not in darkness, so that the day should overtake you unexpectedly.
For you all are children of light and children of day; we are not of night nor of darkness.
So then let us not sleep as the rest do, but let us keep awake and be sober.
For those sleeping sleep at night, and those becoming intoxicated are intoxicated at night.
But we, being of day, let us be sober, having put on breastplate of trust and love, and helmet of hope of salvation/healing.
Because the Divine did not appoint us for condemnation, but for obtaining salvation/healing through our Lord Jesus Messiah,
who died for us, so that whether we are awake or asleep, we may live together with him.
Therefore comfort/encourage one another and build up one another, just as also you are doing.
But we ask you, siblings, to know those laboring among you and standing before you in the Lord and admonishing you,
and to esteem them beyond measure in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
And we appeal to you, siblings: admonish the disorderly, encourage the fainthearted, hold close the weak, be patient toward all.
See that no one repays harmfulness for harmfulness to anyone, but always pursue the good toward one another and toward all.
Rejoice always.
Pray without ceasing.
In everything give thanks, for this is the will of the Divine in Messiah Jesus for you.
Do not quench the Spirit.
Do not despise prophecies,
but test all things; hold fast the beautiful/good.
Hold away from every form of harmfulness.
Now may the Divine of peace himself sanctify you wholly, and may your whole spirit and soul/life and body be kept blameless at the presence/arrival of our Lord Jesus Messiah.
Trustworthy is the One calling you, who also will do it.
Siblings, pray also for us.
Greet all the siblings with a holy kiss.
I adjure you by the Lord that the letter be read to all the siblings.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Messiah be with you.

translation_notes: Armor imagery is sober resilience: breastplate of trust/love and helmet of hope, not aggression toward people.
Spirit, soul/life, and body in 5:23 protects whole-person sanctification rather than body rejection.
Community instructions are practical peace practices: encourage, hold close, be patient, pursue good, test prophecy, do not quench Spirit.
